items.title: ZOETIS DEADLINE: ROSEN, HIGHLY RECOGNIZED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Zoetis Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important July 27 Deadline in Securities Class Action – ZTS
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements about its Companion Animal products during January 2025 to May 2026. The lawsuit claims the company failed to disclose weakening veterinarian adoption of its Librela pain treatment following FDA safety warnings, significant market share losses in Simparica Trio to competitors, and declining sales in dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint. Investors who purchased securities during this period may be eligible for compensation.

items.title: ZOETIS CLASS ACTION DEADLINE MONDAY JULY 27th: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Zoetis, Inc. Investors to Contact the Firm Before the July 27th Lead Plaintiff Deadline
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis, Inc. for allegedly making materially false statements and failing to disclose material adverse facts during the period from January 14, 2025 to May 6, 2026. The complaint alleges that Zoetis concealed weakening veterinarian adoption of its Librela product due to FDA safety warnings, significant market share losses in Simparica Trio to competitors, and declining market share for dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint. Investors have until July 27, 2026 to apply to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

items.title: Deadline Alert: Zoetis Inc. (ZTS) Shareholders Who Lost Money Urged To Contact Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
items.summary: A securities fraud class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making materially false statements and failing to disclose material adverse facts about its business. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased Zoetis securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026. The company's stock declined significantly following three earnings announcements that revealed weakened demand in its Companion Animal portfolio, increased competition, and declining sales of key products including Librela, Simparica Trio, Apoquel, and Cytopoint. The deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion is July 27, 2026.

items.title: ZOETIS DEADLINE: ROSEN, RECOGNIZED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Zoetis Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – ZTS
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements about its Companion Animal products during the period from January 14, 2025 to May 6, 2026. The lawsuit claims the company failed to disclose weakening veterinarian adoption of its Librela pain treatment following FDA safety warnings, significant market share losses in Simparica Trio to competitors, and declining market share in dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint. Investors who purchased Zoetis securities during this period may be eligible for compensation. The lead plaintiff deadline is July 27, 2026.

items.title: Zoetis Inc. (ZTS) Investors: July 27, 2026, Filing Deadline in Securities Fraud Class Action - Contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP
items.summary: A securities fraud class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making materially false statements about product adoption during the class period of January 14, 2025 through May 6, 2026. The lawsuit alleges the company misrepresented the performance of key products including Librela, Simparica Trio, Apoquel, and Cytopoint. Zoetis's stock fell 21.5% on May 7, 2026, following disappointing first quarter results showing significant decline in its Companion Animal business. Investors have until July 27, 2026, to file for lead plaintiff status.

items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements about its Companion Animal products during the period of January 14, 2025 to May 6, 2026. The lawsuit claims the company failed to disclose weakening veterinarian adoption of its flagship products Librela, Simparica Trio, and dermatology treatments Apoquel and Cytopoint due to safety concerns, competition, and market share losses. Investors with losses exceeding $100,000 are encouraged to join the class action before the July 27, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline.

items.title: ZTS Shareholder Alert: Investors With Losses May Seek to Lead the Class Action in Zoetis Inc. Securities Lawsuit - Contact SueWallSt
items.summary: Zoetis Inc. faces a securities class action lawsuit covering January 14, 2025 through May 6, 2026, with CEO Kristin Peck and CFO Wetteny Joseph named as individual defendants. The lawsuit alleges they certified misleading statements about companion animal product performance while safety concerns and competitive losses mounted. Zoetis shares fell 21.5% to $87.31 on May 7, 2026 following corrective disclosures. The lead plaintiff deadline is set for July 27, 2026.

items.summary: Rosen Law Firm is notifying Zoetis Inc. investors who purchased securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026 of an important July 27, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline in an ongoing securities class action. The lawsuit alleges that Zoetis made false statements about market share and sales growth while failing to disclose weakening adoption of flagship products Librela and Simparica Trio, as well as market share losses in dermatology products due to FDA safety warnings and increased competition.

The lawsuit covers investors who purchased Zoetis securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026. The company's stock declined significantly following three earnings announcements that revealed weakened demand in its Companion Animal portfolio, increased competition, and reduced growth outlooks. The deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion is July 27, 2026.

(NYSE: ZTS) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Zoetis made false and misleading statements about market share growth and product adoption while failing to disclose significant market share losses in key products including Librela, Simparica Trio, Apoquel, and Cytopoint due to increased competition and FDA safety warnings. The deadline to apply as lead plaintiff is July 27, 2026.

items.title: ZTS Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Zoetis Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
items.summary: The Schall Law Firm has filed a class action lawsuit against Zoetis Inc. for alleged securities fraud, claiming the company made false and misleading statements regarding its products. The lawsuit alleges that Zoetis failed to disclose weakening prescription growth for Librela following FDA safety warnings about neurological complications in dogs, as well as market share losses for its Trio, Apoquel, and Cytopoint products. Investors who purchased securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026 are encouraged to join the case.

items.title: Veterinary API Manufacturing Market to Reach USD 18.48 Billion by 2035 as Animal Health Investments and Zoonotic Disease Prevention Accelerate – SNS Insider
items.summary: The global Veterinary API Manufacturing Market is projected to grow from USD 7.21 billion in 2025 to USD 18.48 billion by 2035, driven by rising zoonotic disease prevention efforts, increasing pet ownership, and expanding animal healthcare investments. The market is segmented by service type, synthesis type, therapeutic category, and animal type, with North America and Asia-Pacific leading regional growth.

items.title: ZTS Shareholder Alert: July 27, 2026 Lead Plaintiff Deadline in Zoetis Inc. Securities Class Action - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
items.summary: Zoetis Inc. faces a securities class action lawsuit alleging the company misled investors about its Companion Animal portfolio's competitive strength and growth prospects. The stock collapsed 21.5% ($23.91 per share) after four corrective disclosures revealed that key products like Librela, Simparica Trio, and Apoquel/Cytopoint underperformed projections due to safety concerns, intensified competition, and declining veterinarian confidence. The lead plaintiff deadline is July 27, 2026, and the class period covers January 14, 2025 to May 6, 2026.

items.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Zoetis Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. alleging securities fraud for making false statements about product growth and market positioning in its Companion Animal segment. The complaint claims the company failed to disclose weakening adoption of Librela due to FDA safety warnings, significant market share losses for Simparica Trio and dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint to competitors. Investors who purchased Zoetis securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026 are encouraged to join the case.

items.title: ROSEN, NATIONAL INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Zoetis Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – ZTS
items.summary: Rosen Law Firm is encouraging investors who purchased Zoetis Inc. securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026 to join a class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Zoetis made false statements about market share and sales growth while failing to disclose weakening adoption of key products like Librela due to FDA safety warnings, market share losses in Simparica Trio and dermatology products, and declining veterinarian adoption. The lead plaintiff deadline is July 27, 2026.
